pred_reason: The contract exhibits multiple unique security and design issues not directly covered by standard SWC classifications. The unprotected critical function, inconsistent access patterns, and array manipulation risks represent novel vulnerabilities. Evidence: High-risk unprotected registerMintedToken function enables arbitrary token registration; Inconsistent access control between similar functions violates principle of least privilege; Array manipulation edge cases could lead to registry corruption

findings.Description: The registerMintedToken function lacks access control modifiers, allowing any address to register arbitrary tokens. This appears in the smart contract code and was identified in multiple audit tasks.
findings.Impact: Malicious actors could register fake tokens, potentially misleading users and polluting the token registry.

findings.Issue: Inconsistent access control between registration functions
findings.Description: The registerToken function has onlyOwner protection while registerMintedToken has no access control. This inconsistency appears in the smart contract code and was identified during the audit.
findings.Impact: Could lead to security policy confusion and potential unauthorized token registration.

findings.Description: The array deletion mechanism in unregisterToken could theoretically result in edge cases if not properly validated. This appears in the smart contract code and was identified during arithmetic operations analysis.
findings.Impact: Incorrect token tracking or potential registry corruption in edge cases.

findings.Description: The getTokens function contains redundant bounds checking (start >= num and start == num). This appears in the smart contract code and was identified during arithmetic operations review.
findings.Impact: Minor code efficiency issue with no direct security impact.
